system_context.system_description: The inspection system is located at the beginning of a beer can filling process and its goal is to remove dirty beer cans from the input stream. Clean cans should just pass the system without any further action.
system_context.business_goals: To define a domain-specific software architecture (DSSA) that provides a reusable and flexible basis for instantiating measurement systems.

evaluation_setup.evaluation_goal: To evaluate the potential of the designed architecture to reach the software quality requirements and transform it to meet them.

architectures.name: Initial Application Architecture
architectures.description: Designed based on the functional requirements of the system without any explicit design effort with respect to software quality requirements.

key_components.responsibilities: Creates and initializes BeerCan, activates system.
Senses damaged cans, removes beercans, calibrates, measures.

architectures.name: Measurement Systems DSSA
architectures.description: Reengineered architecture incorporating component abstractions, abstract factory, strategy patterns, and prototype pattern.

key_components.responsibilities: Handles the instantiation of actuators, sensors and measurement items.
Generic abstraction for items being measured.
Special case of actuation strategy that stores ideal values as a prototype.

scenarios.scenario_text: Product packaging quality control. For example, sugar packages that are both measured with respected to intact packaging and weight.
Surface finish quality control where multiple algorithms may be used to derive a quality figure to form a basis for decisions.
Quality testing of microprocessors where each processor is either rejected or given a serial number and test data logged in a quality history database in another system.
Product sorting and labelling, e.g., parts are sorted after tolerance levels and labelled in several tolerance categories and are sorted in different storage bins.

Intelligent quality assurance system, e.g., printing quality assurance. The system detects problems with printed results and rejects occasional misprints, but several misprints in a sequence might cause rejection and raising an alarm.
The types of input or output devices used in the system is excluded from the suppliers assortment and need to be changed. The corresponding software needs to be updated.
Advances in technology allows a more accurate or faster calculation to be used. The software needs to be modified to implement new calculation algorithms.
The method for calibration is modified, e.g., from user activation to automated intervals.
The external systems interface for data exchange change. The interfacing system is updated and requires change.
The hardware platform is updated, with new processor and I/O interface.

scenario_classification.rationale: Requires behaviour not present in the initial software architecture.
Requires behaviour not present in the initial software architecture.
Requires behaviour not present in the initial software architecture.
Requires behaviour not present in the initial software architecture.
Requires behaviour not present in the initial software architecture.
Identified as a maintenance scenario where software needs to be updated.
Identified as a maintenance scenario where software needs to be modified.
Identified as a maintenance scenario where methods are modified.
Identified as a maintenance scenario where systems require change.
Identified as a maintenance scenario where the hardware platform is updated.

scenario_evaluations.change_description: Sensing device will have to be more complex and include sophisticated algorithms; actuating device needs to be fed data.
issues_identified: None of the components can be reused.
Impossible to exclude changes to any of the components; possibly all components will have to be updated.
No component can be excluded as not using any hardware specific services from the platform.

scenario_interactions.modularity_implication: Several components dependent of a common state (calibration), making the system tightly coupled and changes complex.

architectural_strengths: Reduced coupling between components by use of the principle programming towards an interface.
Information about concrete types localised in a single entity (ItemFactory).
architectural_weaknesses: M5 is not supported by the DSSA (bad score accepted due to low likelihood).
Reusability is somewhat reduced by the Strategy pattern as smaller number of components are reused as-is.

architecture_comparison.rationale: Satisfies the software quality requirements for reusability and maintainability better than the initial architecture.
Reusability is not too good (classes tightly coupled); Maintainability is not satisfactory (changes not concise).

recommendations: Apply architecture transformations (impose styles, patterns) to improve quality attributes.
Use Strategy pattern to increase conciseness and modularity for calculation and update tasks.
Use Abstract Factory and Prototype patterns to manage component creation and calibration state.

missing_information: Full text of all iterations' analysis.
Exact year of publication (inferred from references).
inference_notes: Classified all scenarios as Indirect because the paper explicitly states they require behavior not present in the initial architecture or involve changes.
Inferred 'Software Engineer' and 'Researcher' roles for participants.
